Workstream lifecycle tracking.description: Each conversation carries a phase (intake, open, in-progress, review, revision, done, abandoned). The router uses the current phase to disambiguate intent, so "looks good" means something different during intake versus after a preview is ready.

Unified feature context assembly.description: A single context assembler gathers the issue, PR, CI status, and preview state for the current workstream, then feeds it to both the PM's agent and the Slack notifier. The same coherent snapshot drives every outgoing message.

LLM-backed conversational notifications.description: Automated Slack updates about PR status and CI events are generated by an LLM specialist rather than synthesized from templates, so the voice stays consistent with the rest of the PM's messages.

Narrated preview events.description: Preview-ready and preview-failed events flow through the event narrator specialist, producing natural-sounding Slack messages rather than machine-formatted status lines.

Per-target language support.description: End-to-end i18n: each target repo can configure the language the PM speaks. Issues, PR comments, and Slack messages all adopt the target's language.

Delegation shifts to the PR once it exis.description: During the first revision, the PM delegates at the issue; once a PR has been opened, further instructions target the PR directly. Removes the old "push to existing branch" workaround and keeps iteration scoped to one living artifact.

Duplicate-PR prevention during feedback.description: When relaying feedback, the delegator instructs OpenCode to push to the existing branch instead of opening a parallel PR — one living PR per workstream, not five.

Delegator scope guard.description: The delegator prompt now refuses to tell OpenCode to create branches or PRs, deferring those to the CI framework. Keeps each piece of the stack responsible for exactly one thing.

Cancel ongoing deployments on new commit.description: If a new commit lands on a PR while the previous deploy is still building, the in-flight deploy is cancelled so the preview always reflects the newest code.

Clean up old bot comments on new deploym.description: Previous progress comments are removed when a fresh deployment starts, keeping the PR conversation focused on the current state rather than a history of stale status updates.

Slack notifications for preview status.description: Preview build, deploy, and ready events post back to the originating Slack thread, so the requester finds out in the same conversation where they asked.

Wait for valid TLS certificate before ma.description: A preview isn't announced as "ready" until the Let's Encrypt certificate for its subdomain resolves. Reviewers never click into a browser warning.

Checkbox-style progress comments.description: Progress on a deployment is shown as a live-updating checklist on the PR, not a stream of new comments — fewer notifications, clearer state.

Slack-GitHub bidirectional integration.description: A top-level @mention in a product Slack channel can create a GitHub issue; GitHub events flow back to the same Slack thread. One workstream, one thread, both sides.

\"via Slack\" backlink on created issues.description: Every issue created from Slack gets a link back to the originating message, so developers can see the full conversation that led to the ticket.

Top-level @mention creates an issue.description: When someone @mentions the PM in a product channel without a thread, the PM treats it as intake and creates a fresh GitHub issue instead of looking for an existing workstream.

Actions API for CI status.description: CI status is pulled from the GitHub Actions API rather than the older Checks API, giving a cleaner view of per-workflow outcomes and fewer edge cases on fine-grained PATs.

Terraform module wires GitHub webhooks.description: The reusable infrastructure module configures all the GitHub webhooks the orchestrator needs when it provisions the EC2 — no manual webhook setup per-repo.

Self-healing reconciliation.description: On startup the orchestrator reconciles its SQLite state against Docker and GitHub, so half-torn-down previews or PRs closed while offline get caught up automatically.

coding-agent: Delegation shifts to the PR once it exists
pr-previews: Cancel ongoing deployments on new commits
Clean up old bot comments on new deployments
Wait for valid TLS certificate before marking ready
